name: Dr Mark Salvatore
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary geochemistry • Surface processes • Earth-Mars analog environments
bio: Mark R. Salvatore is an Associate Professor and Associate Department Chair in the Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University. He is a planetary scientist whose research focuses on the evolution of planetary surfaces, combining geochemical, morphological, and remote sensing approaches to study landscapes on Earth and Mars. Dr. Salvatore integrates fieldwork, laboratory analyses, and satellite data to investigate surface processes in extreme environments, with a particular emphasis on desert and polar regions such as Antarctica’s McMurdo Dry Valleys. His work explores topics including geochemistry, hydrology, and ecosystem dynamics, helping to better understand how planetary surfaces evolve over time. Through interdisciplinary methods that bridge geology, remote sensing, and environmental science, he contributes to advancing knowledge of both Earth analog systems and extraterrestrial environments.
